title: Tomato Transformation Media
author: Alex Rajewski
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.x7dfri6

steps.title: To make 1L, for each media,...
steps.instruction: To make 1L, for each media, dissolve the following components to ~800mL of MilliQ water in a 1L beaker %s %s

steps.title: Bring volume to 1L and adjust pH (±0
steps.instruction: Bring volume to 1L and adjust pH (±0.03) according to the table below: %s %s

steps.title: Divide the volume into two screw-top...
steps.instruction: Divide the volume into two screw-top jars and add the following gelling agent. Note that not all medias require a gelling agent, and that this mass is the TOTAL mass added for 1L. It is not necessary to dissolve this powder; it will dissolve in the autoclave. %s %s

steps.title: Autoclave the media, and allow to cool to ~50º
steps.instruction: Autoclave the media, and allow to cool to ~50º. Add any antibiotics and phytohormones (filter-sterile) according to the table below: %s %s

steps.title: In the sterile hood, dispense the...
steps.instruction: In the sterile hood, dispense the media into the following containers. The liquid media can simply be stored in the screw-top jar. %s %s

steps.title: Once the media has solidified, return...
steps.instruction: Once the media has solidified, return the plates to their sleeves, label them accordingly, and store a 4ºC for up to several months.
